mission: Lane MAP-005: Broad cartographer scan of integrations — Slack, http-recorder, containers, enterprise, script, web

packages.purpose: Slack bot integration via Bolt framework — creates threaded opencode sessions from Slack messages
packages.production_status: isolated — zero runtime imports from any other package

source_files.purpose: Main entry: Slack Bolt app that forwards messages to opencode sessions via SDK. 146 lines, single-file app.

packages.architecture_summary: Standalone Bun process. Creates a Slack Bolt app (Socket Mode) and an opencode SDK server. Maps Slack channel+thread pairs to opencode sessions. Listens for messages and forwards tool updates back to Slack threads. Has no importers — must be run standalone with `bun dev`.
packages.test_coverage: Zero tests. No test directory exists.

security_concerns.detail: Logs full env config on startup showing token presence (lines 11-14). While using `!!` for booleans, reveals whether credentials exist

Logs entire Slack context as JSON on every event — `console.log('📡 Raw Slack event:', JSON.stringify(context, null, 2))`. This dumps raw user messages, channel IDs, and metadata into stdout/logs unstripped
Logs full opencode API response as JSON — `console.log('📤 Opencode response:', JSON.stringify(result, null, 2))`. This sends user prompts and AI responses to stdout
sessions Map grows unbounded — no pruning, no TTL, no cleanup on disconnect. Long-running bots will exhaust memory
No Slack request verification (X-Slack-Signature header checking) visible. The Bot Token and App Token provide auth but the middleware doesn't verify inbound requests came from Slack
No rate limiting on message processing. A flood of Slack messages could overwhelm the opencode server
Tool update failures silently caught with `.catch(() => {})` — no retry, no logging, no backoff

abandonment_indicators: Single-file implementation with no module structure
Zero test coverage
Zero runtime imports from any other package — only referenced in codebase-graph.ts as a label
No production runtime path — must be started manually with `bun dev`
Sessions Map grows unbounded with no cleanup strategy
Verbose debug logging left in production code
Last meaningful git commit: e8f155c1e (re-org), caf2451da (chore: generate)

packages.purpose: HTTP request/response recorder for LLM provider calls. Effect Layer adapter for record/replay testing.
packages.production_status: active — used by llm and opencode test suites (devDependency only)

source_files.purpose: Barrel export — re-exports all public API plus namespaced Cassette, Redactor, HttpRecorder objects
Core record/replay adapter. Provides cassetteLayer() and recordingLayer() Effect layers that intercept HttpClient
Cassette storage backend — Effect Context service with fileSystem and memory adapters. Guards against secret leaks
Effect Schema definitions for cassette JSON format (v1). RequestSnapshot, ResponseSnapshot, HttpInteraction, WebSocketInteraction
Secret redaction primitives — redactUrl() strips credentials/query params, redactHeaders() strips to allow-list, secretFindings() scans for known secret patterns
Composable Redactor abstraction with defaults stack. Supports requestHeaders, responseHeaders, url, body transforms
Request matching with canonical JSON comparison. sequential matching, mismatch diagnostics with safe text output
Shared transport — resolveAutoMode(), makeReplayState() with CI/CD awareness (CI=true forces replay)
WebSocket record/replay — makeWebSocketExecutor() that intercepts open/send/messages/close

consumers.role: test-only — recorded test infrastructure for provider tests

packages.architecture_summary: Testing infrastructure, not a standalone server. Functions as an Effect Layer adapter wrapping HttpClient. 4 modes: auto (CI=replay, else record-if-missing), record, replay, passthrough. In record mode, captures HTTP/WebSocket interactions to JSON cassettes. In replay mode, serves pre-recorded responses and asserts request matching. Redaction pipeline strips secrets before writing cassettes. Designed as a devDependency with zero production footprint.
packages.test_coverage: Low in-package (1 test file). Heavily exercised through consumer tests in packages/llm (6+ test files) and packages/opencode (1 test file).

security_concerns.detail: secretFindings() proactively scans for known secret patterns (Bearer tokens, sk-* keys, AIza* keys, AWS access keys, GitHub tokens, PEM private keys) before writing cassettes. UnsafeCassetteError thrown if secrets detected
envSecrets() scans process.env for entries matching secret key patterns and filters short/safe values to prevent env var leakage into cassettes
safeText() in matching.ts checks for secret patterns before stringifying values for mismatch diagnostics — prevents secrets in test failure output
cassettePath() uses path.join without path validation — if cassette name from user input contains '..', could escape recordings directory. Internal tool so low risk
hasCassetteSync() uses fs.existsSync() — synchronous blocking call

abandonment_indicators: Well-maintained with comprehensive README (209 lines) and clear architecture
Has 217-line README with quickstart, mode table, cassette format docs, redaction config examples
Used by two consumer packages in CI test pipelines
Only 1 substantive git commit (generated import) — but package is stable/mature, not abandoned

packages.purpose: Docker container image build configuration — base images, Bun+Node, Rust toolchains, Tauri Linux builder, and publish images
packages.production_status: active build infrastructure — used in CI/CD pipeline

source_files.purpose: Base Ubuntu 24.04 image with essential build tools
Bun + Node.js on top of base image
Publish image for building and pushing release artifacts
Rust toolchain image for native compilation
Tauri Linux builder with all required system libraries
Build script that orchestrates docker buildx for all images, supports multi-arch (amd64+arm64) and push to ghcr.io/anomalyco

consumers.role: Base images for all GitHub Actions builds

packages.architecture_summary: NOT a code package — a Docker image build configuration. 5 Dockerfiles with a Bun build script that handles multi-arch builds and pushes to GitHub Container Registry. No source code, no runtime — pure container image definitions.
packages.test_coverage: N/A — Docker build configuration, not testable code

security_concerns.detail: Base image is Ubuntu 24.04 — standard, but should be regularly rebuilt for security patches

abandonment_indicators: Active: used by CI/CD pipeline for all GitHub Actions builds

packages.purpose: Enterprise deployment — SolidJS app with Cloudflare Workers support. Teams-focused deployment with session sharing, S3/R2 storage.
packages.production_status: active — deployed on Cloudflare Workers (SST stage: Teams, production)

source_files.purpose: SolidJS root component — i18n, routing, dialog provider, MarkedProvider, locale detection
App-level styles
SolidJS client entry — hydration
SolidJS server entry — SSR
S3/R2 storage adapter — read/write/remove/list/update for JSON objects. Uses aws4fetch for auth. Supports S3 and Cloudflare R2 backends.
Session sharing logic — create/read/sync/remove shares with secret-based auth. Snapshot and compaction support. Zod validation.
Hono-based REST API — share create/sync/data/delete endpoints with OpenAPI docs, Zod validation, CORS
Index/home page route
Share page wrapper
Individual share view page
404 not found page

consumers.role: Listed in codebase-graph.ts as label — no runtime import

packages.architecture_summary: Full-stack SolidJS + Hono app deployed on Cloudflare Workers. Uses @solidjs/start for SSR. Provides session sharing with S3/R2 storage backend. REST API with Zod validation and OpenAPI docs. Secret-based authentication for share access (no user auth). Uses x-forwarded-* headers for URL construction.
packages.test_coverage: Minimal — test directory exists but limited coverage noted

security_concerns.detail: cors() middleware called with NO options — defaults to allowing any origin (*). Enterprise API endpoints can be called from any domain. Should restrict to known origins
Share API uses only a randomUUID secret for authentication — no user identity, no rate limiting, no abuse prevention. Anyone with the share ID+secret can sync/delete
AWS accessKeyId and secretAccessKey read directly from process.env with non-null assertions (!). No credential rotation, no secret manager integration
URL construction trusts x-forwarded-host and x-forwarded-proto headers without validation — could allow host header injection and URL spoofing if not behind a trusted proxy
S3 list response parsed with regex on XML string — fragile and could miss entries with non-standard XML formatting
shareID passed directly from URL param to storage paths without sanitization — potential path traversal if storage adapter doesn't validate

abandonment_indicators: Active deployment target (SST stage: Teams, production)
Has build:cloudflare variant for Cloudflare Workers
Last meaningful changes: e8f155c1e (refactor desktop app init)

packages.purpose: Documentation website — Astro + Starlight docs site. Product docs, quickstart guides, API reference.

source_files.purpose: Astro middleware — locale detection from cookie/Accept-Language, docs alias redirects (/docs/<locale> normalization)
Astro components — Footer, Head, Header, Hero, Lander, Share, SiteTitle, plus share subcomponents
Static/doc page routes
Documentation content
Internationalization support
Stylesheets
TypeScript type definitions
Static assets

packages.architecture_summary: Static documentation site built with Astro + Starlight. Middleware handles i18n (locale detection, docs alias redirects). Content in src/content/. No backend, no API — pure static site.

security_concerns.detail: oc_locale cookie set with SameSite=Lax and Max-Age=31536000 (1 year). No HttpOnly (not needed, not sensitive) and no Secure flag (should be added for HTTPS-only deployments)
docsAlias() redirects based on URL path — redirect target constructed from user-controlled path. Should validate locale values against known set

abandonment_indicators: Active: Astro docs site with locale support and structured content pipeline

packages.purpose: Build/release metadata preload module — NOT a script runner. Provides channel, version, preview, release, team properties for monorepo build scripts.
packages.production_status: active — imported by every build and publish script in the monorepo

source_files.purpose: Preload module: validates Bun version, determines release channel (git branch / env), computes version (npm registry / env), loads team from .github/TEAM_MEMBERS. All logic runs eagerly on import.

packages.architecture_summary: Misleading name — this is NOT a script execution engine. It's a build/release metadata preload singleton. Imported as a side-effecting module at the top of every monorepo build/publish script. Eagerly validates Bun version, discovers release channel, computes version, loads team roster. Exports a single Script const object with computed getters. Zero internal dependencies on other workspace packages.
packages.test_coverage: Zero — no test directory exists. Critical for build pipeline but completely untested.

security_concerns.detail: Script.version fetches https://registry.npmjs.org/opencode-ai/latest and casts response as `any` without validation. Malformed response → undefined version → broken downstream scripts
Git branch name used directly as npm publish tag without sanitization. Could contain spaces/special chars
Every import triggers file reads, git commands, and HTTP fetches with no lazy-load escape. IDE auto-import causes network I/O
Bun version mismatch throws unconditionally — no recovery path for scripts

abandonment_indicators: Single-file, no module structure
Zero tests despite being critical build infrastructure
Inherited from prior project incarnation ('zen', 'ion', SST monorepo era)
Eager side-effect design suggests 'write once, frozen' pattern
Name misleading — 'script' package that doesn't run scripts

source_files.purpose: Hono app with SyncServer DurableObject class, WebSocket sync, JWT verification, GitHub App auth, installation lookup. 391 lines.

security_concerns.detail: GitHub App private key configured via SST Resource.Auth — must ensure proper secret management
JWT verification via createRemoteJWKSet — good practice for token validation

additional_packages_of_interest.purpose: Admin console — auth (OpenAuth), billing (Stripe), analytics, charts, email, support ticket system. SST-deployed Cloudflare Workers app.

security_concerns.detail: Stripe integration for billing — payment processing, must ensure PCI compliance and proper webhook verification
OpenAuth for authentication — critical auth boundary for admin console access

additional_packages_of_interest.purpose: Identity/Auth package — appears to be a placeholder or abandoned directory. No package.json, unclear status.
additional_packages_of_interest.status: orphan — directory exists but no package.json, not in workspaces

consistent_patterns: All packages use tsgo for typecheck (not tsc)
All packages use Bun as runtime
All packages follow effect/context service pattern where applicable
All packages use Zod for schema validation
All packages have private: true (no packages published independently to npm except through monorepo release)

inconsistent_patterns.issue: Single-file app with no module structure — unlike all other packages which are multi-file
Not a code package — Docker build configuration only, no src/ directory
Eager side-effect import pattern — all other packages export functions/classes, not side-effects
These packages are private: false in package.json but are internal monorepo packages — should be private: true
Uses jsxImportSource: 'react' solely for @jsx-email dependency — undocumented workaround
Lists @tsconfig/bun in dependencies but has no tsconfig.json
Only package in the monorepo that has a build step (tsc) — all others use runtime transpilation
No TypeScript project references used anywhere — missed optimization for 16+ interdependent packages
Uses Astro, not SolidJS — only package not using SolidJS for UI
Uses namespaced exports (Share.Storage) — inconsistent with barrel export/re-export pattern used by http-recorder and core

imported_by_core_source: script (build/publish scripts only — not production runtime)
http-recorder (test files only — not production runtime)

commit_details.message: Refactor desktop app initialization and runtime plumbing

git_activity.interpretation: Integration packages are stable — almost no changes in 2026. This is either maturity (http-recorder, containers, script) or neglect (slack).

orphan_directories.detail: Directory exists but has no package.json. Not referenced in root workspaces. Likely abandoned early auth prototype.

explicitly_listed_workspaces: packages/slack (only integration explicitly named in workspace config)

smoking_guns.title: Slack bot is a security liability in its current form
smoking_guns.detail: The Slack integration logs raw user messages, full API responses, and startup config to stdout on every interaction. The sessions Map grows unbounded with no cleanup. There's zero test coverage. It's not imported by any other package — it's a completely isolated one-file script that must be manually started. If deployed, it would leak user conversations into logs and eventually crash from memory exhaustion.
smoking_guns.recommendation: Either add structured logging (strip user content), implement session TTL/cleanup, add tests, and integrate into CI/CD — or archive as a reference implementation.

smoking_guns.title: Enterprise API has permissive CORS and no user authentication
smoking_guns.detail: The enterprise share API uses cors() with no options (allows any origin) and authenticates with only a randomUUID secret — no user identity, no rate limiting, no abuse prevention. Combined with x-forwarded-host header trust for URL construction, this creates a host header injection vector.
smoking_guns.recommendation: Restrict CORS to known origins, add user authentication layer, validate x-forwarded-* headers against allowed proxy ranges.

smoking_guns.title: Zero integration packages are imported by opencode production runtime
smoking_guns.detail: Not one of the 6 integration packages (slack, http-recorder, containers, enterprise, script, web) is imported by packages/opencode/src production code. http-recorder and script are only used in test/build scripts. All integrations are standalone, build-time, or test-time only.
smoking_guns.recommendation: If these are 'integrations,' their integration surface with the core engine is zero at runtime. Consider whether to formalize integration contracts or archive the disconnected ones.

smoking_guns.title: @opencode-ai/script is a misnamed build metadata singleton, not a script engine
smoking_guns.detail: The 'script' package does not provide any scripting capability. It's a build-time preload module that runs eagerly on import and exports version/channel/team metadata. It has zero tests despite being critical to 7+ build scripts. The naming is misleading and suggests a different purpose than reality.
smoking_guns.recommendation: Either rename to @opencode-ai/build-meta or add tests and lazy-load support.

smoking_guns.title: packages/identity is an orphan directory with no package.json
smoking_guns.detail: The identity directory exists in the monorepo but has no package.json and is not referenced in any workspace configuration. It appears to be a remnant of an early auth prototype that was abandoned.
smoking_guns.recommendation: Either implement or remove the directory to avoid confusion.

smoking_guns.title: Inconsistent namespace export patterns across integrations
smoking_guns.detail: http-recorder uses barrel re-exports with namespaced objects (Cassette, Redactor, HttpRecorder). Enterprise uses TypeScript namespaces (Share.Storage, Share.Errors). Script exports a singleton const. Slack exports nothing — it's a side-effect script. No consistent integration interface pattern exists.
smoking_guns.recommendation: Adopt a common effect/context service pattern for all integrations, similar to http-recorder.

smoking_guns.title: http-recorder is a well-architected testing utility with strong security posture
smoking_guns.detail: The http-recorder package demonstrates excellent engineering: Effect Layer architecture, composable redactors, secret pattern scanning before cassette writes, safe text in mismatch diffs, CI/CD-aware mode resolution, and comprehensive documentation. It's the benchmark for how integrations should be built in this codebase.
smoking_guns.recommendation: Use http-recorder as the reference pattern for future integrations.

pre_existing_findings_incorporated.summary: Plugin API has zero sandboxing — 5 critical vulnerabilities
pre_existing_findings_incorporated.relevance: Related to SG-002, SG-001 — integration packages similarly lack security hardening
SLACK_BOT_TOKEN and enterprise storage credentials directly referenced in secrets surface map
